content.claim: Poland's 1999 reform introducing a comprehensive three-year lower-secondary stage (gimnazjum) correlated with PISA gains between 2000–2015 and documented reductions in socio-economic performance gaps, particularly benefiting formerly vocational-track students.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Correlation is not causal evidence; other concurrent policy changes are not isolated
The cohort that improved had experienced only the introduction of gimnazjum, not a full steady-state system under that structure
Analogy to Hungary's optional-track system is indirect because Poland reformed the age of universal transition rather than embedding optional early-exit tracks
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
